joyful.name: Joyful Mysteries
descriptions: The Angel Gabriel announces to Mary that she will conceive the Son of God by the Holy Spirit.
Mary visits her cousin Elizabeth, whose child leaps for joy at the presence of the Lord.
Jesus is born in a manger in Bethlehem; angels sing glory and shepherds come to adore him.
Mary and Joseph present the infant Jesus in the Temple; Simeon proclaims him the Light of the nations.
After three days, Jesus is found in the Temple among the teachers, astonishing all with his wisdom.

sorrowful.name: Sorrowful Mysteries
descriptions: Jesus prays in Gethsemane in agony, sweating blood, yet surrenders fully to the Father's will.
Bound to a pillar, Jesus is brutally scourged; he endures every blow in patient silence.
Soldiers press a crown of thorns onto Jesus and mock him as king; he bears it with meekness.
Weakened and bleeding, Jesus carries the heavy cross through Jerusalem toward Calvary.
Jesus is crucified and dies, offering forgiveness even to those who condemned him.

glorious.name: Glorious Mysteries
decades: The Descent of the Holy Spirit
descriptions: On the third day, Jesus rises from the dead, victorious over sin and death forever.
Forty days after the Resurrection, Jesus ascends to heaven and sits at the right hand of the Father.
The Holy Spirit descends on Mary and the Apostles at Pentecost, giving birth to the Church.
At the end of her earthly life, Mary is taken body and soul into the glory of heaven.
Mary is crowned Queen of Heaven and Earth, interceding continually for all of humanity.

luminous.name: Luminous Mysteries
descriptions: Jesus is baptized by John in the Jordan; the Father declares him his beloved Son.
At Mary's request, Jesus transforms water into wine at Cana, performing his first miracle.
Jesus proclaims the Kingdom of God and invites all to conversion.
Before Peter, James, and John, Jesus is transfigured, revealing his divine glory on Mount Tabor.
At the Last Supper, Jesus institutes the Eucharist, giving his body and blood as food for eternal life.
